def: adj. Contract. of بيـﮩوده behūda, q. v.
adj. contract. of be-hūda, q.v.
s.f. Absurdity, foolishness, folly, foolery; frivolity nonsense, vanity, unprofitableness.

adj. 1. Senseless; foolish; silly; stupid; nonsensical; absurd. 2. Idle; fruitless; vain; unprofitable. 3. Foul; gross; obscene; immoral; indecent; impolite. behūdah bāteṅ , n. f. Indecent language. behūdah taqrīr , n. f. Idle disputation; fiddlefaddle. behūdah uzr , n. m. An idle or absurd plea or excuse. behūdah uzr karnā , v. n. To make a vain objection. behūdah-go , n. m. A foul-mouthed fellow; a coarse or obscene talker ( bakkī ). behūdah-goī , n. f. Nonsense; obscene or idle talk ( bakwās ).
more: adj. Absurd, vain, foolish, idle, fruitless, unprofitable. بيـﮩوده گو behūda-go, A talker of nonsense, a babbler. بيـﮩوده گوٴي behūda-go-ī, s. f. Talking nonsense, nonsense.

def: n. f. Absurdity; foolishness; frivolity; nonsense; foolery.
more: s. f. Absurdity, nonsense, vanity, unprofitableness.

def: Poet. contrac. of be-hosh, q.v.s.v. be.
s. f. See بي هوشي be-hosh-ī, under بي be.

adj. 1. Without self-possession, control, or authority; incompetent. 2. Constrained; forced; compelled. be-ikhtyār , adv. 1. Nolens volens ; in spite of oneself. 2. Involuntarily; spontaneously; naturally.
n. f. Want of power; incompetency; disability; helplessness.
adj. 1. Without religion or conscience; unprincipled; corrupt. 2. Dishonest; fraudulent; false; unscrupulous; crooked. 3. Faithless; perfidious; slippery; treacherous ( dagā-bāz ).

n. f. lit . Infidelity. Irreligion; dishonesty; breach of trust; unfair dealing; unfairness. 1. Be-ímānī kā mūṅh kālā . Prov. Roguery has a black face. 2. Yā be-īmānī, terā āsrā! Prov. In fraud is my hope. be-īmānī kar'nā , v. n. 1. To play false; cheat; defraud. 2. To misappropriate; embezzle; peculate.
adj. Unjust. See انيائي (1-3).
n. f. Injustice. See انياو.
adj. Endless. See بےحد
n. f. Mismanagement. See بدانتظامي

1. Out of place; misplaced; irrelevant. 2. Improper; unlawful; unjustifiable; illegal. 3. Unreasonable; contrary to reason; unsound; absurd. 4. Inaccurate; wrong; objectionable. be-jā , adv. Improperly; injudiciously; wrongly. be-jā kharch, yā sarf , n. m. Needless expense; extravagance; waste; dissipation.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بیجا (bējā).

def: adj. Amiss.
s.m. Butt or mark for archers.
more: s. m. A butt or mark for archers.

def: s.f.m. Mixed crop, mixed grain (generally wheat and barley, or grain and barley).
more: n. f. H. bi two, joṛ join. Mixed corn, generally gram and barley. Saukan din din chāṅval rāṅdhe, maiṅ bejhaṛ nā khāūṅgī. Jo tauṅ bejhaṛ lāë bisā-ke, us ne bār khiṅdāūṅgī . Rus. song. My co-wife every day eats rice, I will not eat mixed grain, If you will buy me such, I vow, it out shall go again. bejhṛā , adj. Of a mixed race; mongrel.

def: s.f.m. Mixed crop, mixed grain (generally wheat and barley, or grain and barley).

s.f.m. Mixed crop, mixed grain (generally wheat and barley, or grain and barley).

s. f. Agitation (of mind).
adj. Restless ( be-chain ).

adj. Destitute.

s. f. 1. Root. 2. Origin, foundation. بيخ کن beḵẖ-kan, An extirpator, exterminator; a judge (as extirpating thieves); a thief (as destroying the subjects). بيخ کني beḵẖ-kan-ī, s. f. Extirpation, extermination, eradication.

def: s. m. Decoration, dress. See بهيس bhes, and ويش vesh̤.
s.m. = bhes, q.v. s.m. & f. Buffalo (female).
s.m. = bhes, q.v. s.m. & f. Buffalo (female).
adj. Without money for expenses.
adj. Without understanding, stupid.
adj. Senseless. See az-khud raftah . Enraptured; transported; intoxicated.
n. f. 1. Insensibility; stupefaction. 2. Rapture; transport; ecstasy.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بیخْوَدِی (bēxwadī).

def: adj. 1. Worthless ( birthā ). 2. Without work or employment; unemployed; idle; doing nothing. Be-kār ma-bāsh, kuchh kīyā kar. Kapṛe hī udheṛ-kar sīyā kar . Prov. Be not idle, something do. (Parodied as follows). Unrip your clothes and stitch them o'er anew. 3. Inoperative; ineffective; ineffectual; inefficient. 4. Without force; invalid; nugatory; null. be-kār karnā , v. a. 1. To thwart; bulk; frustrate; disconcert; defeat; undo. 2. To render useless or unserviceable; break; destroy. 3. To render defective; invalidate; undo.
s.m. The Arabian jasmine, Jasminum zambac; — vessel, well; spot in which the receiving pans are placed when sugar is manufactured
subs: s.m. Jasmine shrub or creeper (see s.v. bel, 'creeper').
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀯𑁂𑀮𑁆𑀮𑀻 (vĕllī), from Sanskrit वेल्लि (velli). Cognate with Gujarati વેલ (vel), Marathi वेल (vel), Punjabi ویل (vel) / ਵੇਲ (vel).

def: s.m. Spade, shovel, hoe, mattock, pickaxe; line of a road or a walk marked out with a spade; border; pole for directing a boat (cf. ballī)
subs: s.m. Digger, one who uses a spade, pioneer.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀯𑁂𑀮𑁆𑀮𑀻 (vĕllī), from Sanskrit वेल्लि (velli). Cognate with Gujarati વેલ (vel), Marathi वेल (vel), Punjabi ویل (vel) / ਵੇਲ (vel).

def: s.f. Any climbing or creeping plant, creeper, climber, vine; tendril; offspring, issue, descendants; strings, rows, or festoons of leaves, &c. hung round a room or tent; embroidery, flowered tissue; cutaneous disease, eruption; — money given by the spectators to a dancing-girl, or to a player
subs: s.m. Shrubs and creepers; flowers or embroidery (on cloth, &c.): flowered border
v.n. To bear fruit, have offspring; to attain one's precise or utmost wish
v.n. A creeper to climb to the top (of); to reach the top or summit (of); to come to perfection; to flourish, prosper.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀯𑁂𑀮𑁆𑀮𑀻 (vĕllī), from Sanskrit वेल्लि (velli). Cognate with Gujarati વેલ (vel), Marathi वेल (vel), Punjabi ویل (vel) / ਵੇਲ (vel).

def: s.m. The wood-apple or Bel-tree, Ӕgle marmelos, and its fruit
more: n. f. S. वल्लि, Pr. bellī from वल्ल् to move. 1. A creeping plant; creeper; climber; parasite. Jaṛ kaṭe aur bel baṛhe . Prov. Cutting the root makes a tall tree. (Applied to eunuchs and tall persons). 2. Wooden apple; Aegle marmelos. Fac . the head. 3. Generation; offspring ( baṛhotrī ). Nanne jī kī bel! Benediction. Blest be the young ones with fair progeny! 4. A line of road marked out with a spade. 5. Tape; border. 6. Embroidery; flowered tissue. 7. A pole for directing a boat. 8. A disease; an eruption. 9. The place where the receiving-pans of a sugar-factory are placed. 10. Money given to dancing girls, players, barbers, etc. on festive occasions ( bel-baṭṭā ). 1. Kab dādā mareṅge aur kab bel baṭegī? Prov. When will the grandsire die, when will the funeral gifts be made? 2. Nācheṅ bajā-ke tālyāṅ, aur gāweṅ hījṛe, Le le-ke bel bhāo bhī batlāweṅ hījṛe . Nazīr. See now these eunuchs st…
1. (for विल्व ) s. m. The name of a fruit. (Cratæva or Ӕgle marmelos). 2. (for वल्लि ) s. f. A creeper, climber; tendril (of a vine).
s. m. The name of a flower. (Jasminum zambac). بيل پهلني bel phalne, To attain oneʼs precise or utmost wish.
subs: bel-pattar, s.m., bel-pattī, s.f. Leaf of the Bel-tree (employed as an offering to Śiva)
s.m. Fruit of the Bel-tree, wood-apple
s.f. Pulp of the Bel or wood-apple (used medicinally for diarrhœa and dysentery).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀯𑁂𑀮𑁆𑀮𑀻 (vĕllī), from Sanskrit वेल्लि (velli). Cognate with Gujarati વેલ (vel), Marathi वेल (vel), Punjabi ویل (vel) / ਵੇਲ (vel).

def: s.f. Time, while, period, moment, season.
s.m. The shrub or flower Jasminum zambac; shallow cup (syn. kaṭorā); an instrument of music resembling a fiddle.
more: n. m. S. वेला from वेल् to go. 1. Time ( waqt ); moment; season; period. 2. A cup ( kaṭorā ). 3. A Native harp. 4. A species of Jasmine.

n. m. A bag of money carried in the hand of a person of rank for distribution among the poor. belā-bardār , n. m. One of the retinue of a great man who scatters money among the populace. belā kharch , n. m. 1. Alms; charity. 2. Eleemosynary charges or account. belā baṭnā , v. n. To be distributed (alms). belā bāṅṭ'nā , v. a. To give alms.
s. f. A while, a time.
s. f. 1. Name of a shrub. (Jasminum zambac). 2. A cup. 3. An instrument of musick resembling a fiddle.
s. m. Money for charity. بيلابردار belābardār, One of the retinue of a great man who scatters money among the people.

def: s.f. The iron point of an arrow.
more: s. f. 1. A small mattock. 2. The iron point of an arrow.

def: s.m. A cylinder of wood, metal, or stone for levelling walks, spreading dough, or inking the surface of type; roller; rolling-pin.
more: n. m. S. वेल् to move. A cylinder of wood, metal, or stone for levelling walks, spreading dough, and inking the face of the types; a roller; rolling-pin.
s. m. A rolling pin. ¶ belani, A branch.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit वेल्लन (vellana, “shaking; rolling pin”). Cognate with Bengali বেলন (belon), Gujarati વેલણ (velaṇ), Punjabi ویلْݨا (velṇā).

def: v.t. (Prov.), To collect cattle.

s.f. The iron point of an arrow.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بیلْچَه (bēlča), diminutive of بیل (bēl), from Middle Persian [script needed] (byl /⁠bēl⁠/).

def: s.f. The iron point of an arrow.
n. m. A hoe; a small mattock or spade.
more: (dimin. of بيل) s. m. A hoe, a spade, a small mattock.

def: n. m. A laborer whose work is to dig or delve.
more: s. m. A pioneer, a digger, user of a spade.

def: n. f. The pulp of the bel , used in diarrhæa and dysentry. bel maṅḍhe chaṛhnā , v. n. To come to perfection; prosper; reach the zenith or highest point. Yeh bel maṅḍhe chaṛhtī nazar nahīṅ ātī . Prov. This climber does not seem likely to get to the top. chaltī bel , n. f. lit . A climbing creeper. At the festival of Sankrat, q. v. Hindu women distribute five cocoanuts or other fruits among five women, each of whom is henceforth bound to distribute so many fruits among other women, and thus the process of multiplication goes on.
s.f. Creeper, &c. ( = bel, q.v.). s.m. The wood-apple or Bel-tree, Ӕgle marmelos, and its fruit
more: n. m. S. बलि powerful, P. wālī owner. Preserver; supporter; protector. Maiṅ ghar se chalī akelī, na lānī saṅg sahelī, Merā dātā haiyā belī, maiṅ ḍālī gal bich selī . Wom. I left my home alone, friends of my own I'd none, God guards the friendless one, the ascetic's garb I'll don.
s. f. A creeper, climber; tendril (of a vine).
subs: bel-pattar, s.m., bel-pattī, s.f. Leaf of the Bel-tree (employed as an offering to Śiva)
s.m. Fruit of the Bel-tree, wood-apple
s.f. Pulp of the Bel or wood-apple (used medicinally for diarrhœa and dysentery).

def: s.m. Preserver, guardian, protector, supporter (applied to the Deity).
s.m. Cattle-breedor; grazier.
v.t. To spread out, roll into a flat cake, to laminate.

1. s. m. A rolling pin. 2. v. a. To spread out, to laminate.

adj. & s.m. Rolling; a roller.

subs: s.m. Brother born of a different mother by the same father, half-brother (by the father's side).

def: s.m. Ant's nest.

n. m. A. minnat favour. A term used by bhaṅgaṛs for a bicornous instrument made of wood, with a cloth spread over it, for straining bhaṅg (liquor), so called because a man, without assistance from others, can himself strain the liquor.
s.f. Reed, bamboo; reed-pipe, pipe, flute, fife.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀬𑀡 (vayaṇa) Sanskrit वचन (vacana).

more: n. m. S. व्यजन, , Pr. viano . 1. A fan (بيجنا). 2. A grass called khas; Andropogon muricatum . Wat.
n. m. H. bindī spangle. Ornaments worn by women on the forehead, or figures painted on it with a colored earth or unguent; an ornament.

n. m. See اَزاد (2).

ety: fr. S. वे; cf. bīṅḍī, benī
def: v.t. To plait, braid; to fold (beṅḍhnā).
v.t. = beṅdhnā, q.v. v.t. To plait, braid; to fold (beṅḍhnā).

s. m. A frog, paddock, toad: see بهيک bhek. 2. byang (for व्यंग्य ) Sarcasm, covert but intelligible expression of suspicion or contempt.

def: s.m. Grain advanced to cultivators, seed lent for sowing.

s.f. Braided hair, the hair twisted into a single unornamented braid and allowed to fall on the back (as worn by widows and women whose husbands are absent, as a token of mourning; — syn. bīṅḍā); the conflux or meeting of two or more streams in a common point of union (as that of the Ganges and Jamna at Allahabad)
more: s. f. The hair twisted behind. See بينڐي bīnḍī.
subs: s.m. A particular square-shaped idol or stone image of a god at Allahabad; a proper name.

def: s.f. Dim. of benā, q.v.
adj. Independent; free; free from want. Uskī zāt be-niyāz hai . God is all-sufficient.

s.m. = binjan, q.v. s.m. Anything used in dressing or preparing food, seasoning, relish, sauce, condiment; vegetables (dressed with butter, or ghī, and added to flesh or fish); — (in Gram.) a consonant (as marking or distinguishing sound).
s.f. Cane, &c. = bet, q.v. s.f. Cane, ratan, Calamus rotang; blow or stroke with a cane
s.m. The circular or turned handle of any weapon or instrument; handle.

adj. Unveiled; uncovered; naked; exposed. be-pard'gī , n. f. 1. The absence of a screen or covering and consequent exposure before strange men. 2. Disgrace; dishonor. dar-pardah , adv. In private; sub rosa; secretly.
adj. 1. Careless; heedless. 2. Independent; unconcerned; free ( āzād ).
n. f. 1. Carelessness; heedlessness; indifference; disregard. 2. Negligence; habitual neglect; inattention; unconcern. 3. Independence; freedom.
adj. lit . having no spiritual guide ( ni-gurā ). 1. Inexorable; unreconcilable. 2. Unfeeling; selfish; cruel; pitiless.
s.f. Time, turn, bout, vicissitude; space of time, interval; delay, while (syn. bār)
subs: adv. Time after time, again and again, repeatedly, frequently, often.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بیر (bīr), from Middle Persian wyl (wīr), from Proto-Indo-European *wiHrós. Cognate with Avestan 𐬬𐬍𐬭𐬀 (vīra), Sanskrit वीर (vīra, “man (especially a brave one), chief, husband”), Northern Kurdish wêrek (“courageous”), Kashmiri ویٖر (vīr, “hero”), Ossetian ир (ir), Latin vir (“man”) Lithuanian výras, and English wer.

def: s.m. Enclosure, fence, &c. ( = beṛā, beṛhā, q.v.)
more: s. m. An enclosure. بيڙبندي کرنا beṛ bandī karnā, To enclose a field.

def: Rus. adv. Often. See باربار
more: adv. Often, frequently, repeatedly, again and again.

def: s.m. The jujube tree, Zizyphus jujuba, or Z. scandens; the fruit of the jujube, plum; the lote-fruit, Rhamnus zizyphus (A. syn. kunār)
more: n. m. S. बदर, Pr. voram jujube. A plum; jujube; zizyphus jujuba . 1. Jhāṛī būṭī kā haī ber, khaṭ-miṭhṛā hai ber! Delhi street cry. Wild bush plums, plums both sweet and tart, ho! 2. Lāl phal kāṅṭe ladā khāve jag sansār, Ber ber maiṅ kahat hūṅ, būjhe kyoṅ na, gaṅvār? Riddle. Ans. Ber plum. ber guṭhlī, lit . Plum and seed. Every thing; hodge podge; good and bad. beroṅ meṅ guṭhlīyāṅ milānā , v. a. 1. To complicate; confuse; confound. 2. To spoil; mar; raise the old difficulties. Ber meṅ guṭhlī milāwe hai ye chodde kā amīn, Gāoṅ kī nāpī paṛī hai sīr apne hāth se . Dalmīr. The seed he mixes with the plums, this w—son dog amīn , Measuring the land with his own hand he must again begin.
Rus. n. f. Delay; turn. See بار
1. s. m. (for वदर ) The name of a fruit, that of the jujube (Zizyphus jujuba); also the name of the tree. 2. (for वार or वेला ) Time, turn, bout, vicissitude, delay, while.
subs: s.m. lit. 'Plum and seed'; the whole, everything, all together, good and bad; hodge-podge
To make a mess (of), to spoil, complicate, confuse.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بیر (bīr), from Middle Persian wyl (wīr), from Proto-Indo-European *wiHrós. Cognate with Avestan 𐬬𐬍𐬭𐬀 (vīra), Sanskrit वीर (vīra, “man (especially a brave one), chief, husband”), Northern Kurdish wêrek (“courageous”), Kashmiri ویٖر (vīr, “hero”), Ossetian ир (ir), Latin vir (“man”) Lithuanian výras, and English wer.

def: s.m. Enclosure, yard, court; fence, hedge, paling, railing ( = beṛhā); siege, blockade, body of soldiers, troop, army; band, association, company, party
more: s. m. 1. A raft, a float. 2. The raft which is floated by the Muhammadans in honour of ḵẖẉāja ḵẖiẓr. (Besides the anniversary beṛa offered to propitiate him, hundreds of smaller ones with lamps may be seen on Thursdays of the month bhādoṅ particularly, which are offered by the Muhammadans to this saint). بيڙاباندهنا beṛa bāndhnā, v. a. To collect a crowd. بيڙاپارکرنايالگانا beṛā pār karnā or lagānā, To relieve from distress, to remove difficulties, to help one through a business ( lit. to ferry over a raft). بيڙاپارهونا beṛā pār honā, To obtain deliverance from misfortune or distress, to succeed.
s. (dakh.) Surrounding, siege, blockade ( Harris ): see بيڙ beṛ and گهيرا gherā.
subs: (-kā), To form an army, collect a body or crowd, &c.
ex: چین کا بیڑا اس وقت بحر الکاہل میں ہے۔
cīn kā beṛā is vaqt bahar-ul-kāhil mẽ hai.
China's fleet is currently in the Pacific Ocean.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀩𑁂𑀟𑀕 (beḍaga), from Sanskrit बेडक (beḍaka), बेडा (beḍā). Cognate to Sindhi ٻِيڙِي (ḇīṛī).

def: s.m. Raft, float; boat; fleet (of boats or ships); timber formed into a raft to be brought down a river; the raft floated by the Mohammadans in honour of Ḵẖẉāja Ḵẖiẓr. (Besides the anniversary beṛā offered to propitiate him, hundreds of smaller ones with lamps may be seen on Thursdays of the month Bhādoṅ particularly, which are offered by the Mohammadans to this saint)
subs: beṛā pār lagānā, v.t. To ferry over a raft or boat; to help one over (a difficulty), remove the difficulties (of, -kā)
v.n. To tide over a difficulty, misfortune, or distress; to get well through a business, to be successful.
ex: چین کا بیڑا اس وقت بحر الکاہل میں ہے۔
cīn kā beṛā is vaqt bahar-ul-kāhil mẽ hai.
China's fleet is currently in the Pacific Ocean.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀩𑁂𑀟𑀕 (beḍaga), from Sanskrit बेडक (beḍaka), बेडा (beḍā). Cognate to Sindhi ٻِيڙِي (ḇīṛī).

def: adj. = birānā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Of, or relating to, or belonging to, another; another's; strange, foreign; extraneous, extrinsic (cf. P. bīrūnī).
s.m. Grove of ber trees (see ber).
adj. 1. Without fibre (a good mango). 2. ( Slang ) A beardless youth; a boy.
s.m. Surrounding, &c. (see beṛhnā); cattle surrounded and carried off (by an enemy) by force
subs: v.t. To carry off cattle by force.

def: s.m. Enclosure; fence, &c. = beṛā, q.v. s.m. Enclosure, yard, court; fence, hedge, paling, railing ( = beṛhā); siege, blockade, body of soldiers, troop, army; band, association, company, party
more: 1. adj. Crooked. 2. s. m. Paling, railing.
subs: (-kā), To form an army, collect a body or crowd, &c.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Crooked; zigzag; — beṛhe, adv. Crookedly, zigzag (cf. beṅḍā).
s.f. = beṛwīṅ, q.v. s.f. Bread or cakes made of flour mixed or filled with pulse or meal (syn. beṛhaʼīṅ; pīṭhī; kaćaurī).
s.f. = beṛwīṅ, q.v. s.f. Bread or cakes made of flour mixed or filled with pulse or meal (syn. beṛhaʼīṅ; pīṭhī; kaćaurī).
v.t. To enclose with a fence; to envelop; to surround, besiege; to drive or carry off cattle (as in a foray); to pound (cattle, &c.).
more: v. a. 1. To enclose with a fence, to surround. 2. To pound (cattle, &c.). 3. To drive away cattle.

def: s.f. Name of a class of singers in Mirzapur.
n. f. A sect of songstresses in Mirzapore.
s.f. The jujube tree, Zizyphus jujuba ( = ber).
more: n. f. 1. Fetters; irons fastened to the legs of prisoners, or of a quadruped. 2. Thread tied on the ankles; an iron anklet. 3. ( Met .) Nuptial ties; wedlock. 4. Fetters; shackles; trammels; restraint. beṛī paṛnā, beṛyāṅ paṛnā , v. n. 1. To be put in irons, to be chained, imprisoned. 2. To be tied in wedlock. beṛ'yāṅ pahnānā, yā ḍālnā , v. a. To cause to be put in chains. beṛyaṅ kaṭnā , v. n. 1. To be taken off (one's chains). 2. To be freed ( āzād honā ), discharged, released. 3. To be freed from any restraint, obstacle, or impediment.
s. f. The tree of Zizyphus jujuba.

def: s.f. Ring worn round the ankle; irons fastened to the legs of criminals, or of a quadruped; fetters; thread tied on the ankles, a dark blue thread or cord tied round the ankles of children (as a charm); (met.) nuptial tie, wedlock; — basket used to irrigate fields with
more: s. f. 1. Irons fastened to the legs (of criminals, elephants, &c.), fetters. 2. A basket used to irrigate fields with.
s. f. (dakh.) A ring worn round the ankle ( Qanoon-e-islam ).
subs: (-meṅ), beṛiyāṅ pahnānā, beṛiyāṅ ḍālnā, To put fetters (on the feet of), to put in chains; to cause to be put in chains; — beṛiyāṅ paṛnā, v.n. To be put in irons; to be chained; to be tied in wedlock
(-kī), To have the fetters or chains taken off; to be freed from restraint, to be discharged or released.

def: s.m. Name of a caste of Hindūs.
more: s. m. 1. The name of a cast of Hindūs. 2. S̱. dimin. of بيڙا beṛā, q. v.

def: s.f. dim. of beṛā, q.v.
s.m. Sum total, entire revenue of a farm; a kind of government tax.
s.f. Bread or cakes made of flour mixed or filled with pulse or meal (syn. beṛhaʼīṅ; pīṭhī; kaćaurī).
prep. & adv. Without, on the outside, out ( = bāhar)
more: prep. Without. berūṅjāt , n. m. The suburbs; the country.
prep. Without, on the outside, out. بيرونجات berūn-jāt, Country (opposed to town). بيرونجاتي berūn-jāt-ī, Rustick, belonging to a village or the country, a countryman.
subs: vulg. berūn-jāt, s.m. Suburbs; country (in opp. to town)
adj. Of or pertaining to the suburbs or country, rustic, rural; country-bred; — s.m. Rustic, country-man.

def: adj. Outer, external, &c. = bāharī, q.v. adj. & s.m. Outward, outer, exterior, external; extraneous, extrinsic, strange, foreign; — stranger, foreigner; outsider ( = ūparī); upstart.
s.f. Bread or cakes made of flour mixed or filled with pulse or meal (syn. beṛhaʼīṅ; pīṭhī; kaćaurī).
n. f. A flour cake filled with pulse meal.
= besh; and bhes; qq.v. adj. More; greater; good, proper, well; better, superior, excellent, preferable; elegant; delightful
subs: adj. & s.m. Very much, a great deal
besh qīmat, adj. Of great price or value, costly, precious
adj. & adv. Not much, moderate quantity; more or less.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀻𑀲 (vīsa), from Sanskrit विं॒श॒ति (viṃśatí).

def: s.m. = bhes, q.v. s.m. & f. Buffalo (female).
s. f. Impatience.
s.m. Flour or meal of pulse (particularly of ćanā; commonly used as a substitute for soap).
more: n. m. S. पेषण flour. The flour or meal of dāl (pulse), used instead of soap.
s. m. The flower or meal of pulse (particularly of chanā, Cicer arietinum).

def: adj. & s.f. Made of, or mixed with, besan; — bread, &c. made of besan and wheat-flour.
more: n. f. Bread, etc. made of besan and wheat-meal.
adj. Made of or mixed with بيسن besan, q. v.
s. f. A female of the بيس bais cast.

def: s.m. A mule.
more: s. f. The small ring worn in the nose.

def: s.m. Small heavy nose-ring (syn. nak-besar).
n. f. 1. Unveiling; exposure. 2. Immodesty; boldness. 3. Dishonor; disgrace. be-satrī be-hūdah , G. G. Public nuisance or indecent exposure of the person.
adj. More; greater; good, proper, well; better, superior, excellent, preferable; elegant; delightful
more: adj. 1. Better; superior; preferable ( achchhā ). 2. Superior (a card).
n. m. S. बैश्य A Hindu of the third caste or order whose occupations are agriculture and trade.

adj. 1. More. 2. Good, proper, well, excellent, elegant, delightful. بيش بـﮩا besh-bahā, or بيش قيمت besh-ḳīmat, Of great price or value, costly, precious. بيش ازبيش besh az besh, Very much, a great deal.
subs: adj. & s.m. Very much, a great deal
besh qīmat, adj. Of great price or value, costly, precious
adj. & adv. Not much, moderate quantity; more or less.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بیش (bēš).

def: s.m. See بيشه besha.

ety: Pehl. veshak; Zend varesha; S. vr̤iksha
def: s.m. Forest, jungle; desert, wilderness.
adv. Undoubtly ( bilā-shak ).
ex: be śak yih merī qamīz hai.
For sure, this is my shirt.
آپ بیشک اس طرف سے چلے جائیں۔
āp be śak is taraf se cale jāẽ.
You can go this way if you like.
wety: From بے (be, “without”) + شک (śak, “doubt”). Compare Persian بلا شک (belâ šak, “undoubtedly”).

def: s. f. Dishonour, indignity.
adj. Shameless ( be-hayā ); brazen-faced. besharmī ; H. nilajtā , n. f. Shamelessness (بيحيائي). jāë sharm hai! sharm kī bāt hai! It is a shame! fie! shame on you! for shame.
s.f. Excess, surplus, increase; premium; increased assessment
more: n. f. 1. Excess; premium (برَهوتري 1, 2). beshī-i-lagān , n. m. Enhancement of rent.
subs: s.f. Increase of revenue on that of the year preceding
s.f. Increase in the lands under cultivation

def: adj. Not much, a moderate quantity, more or less.
adj. comp. More, most, mostly, for the most part, usually, exceeding, better, rather.
adj. & adv. More, most; better; exceeding; — mostly, for the most part, usually, generally; rather (syn. aks̤ar).
s.m. Name of a celebrated mountain in Persia which Farhād dug through at the command of his mistress Shīrīn.
more: n. prop. A celebrated mountain in Persia, which Farhād dug through at the command of his mistress Shīrīn.

def: s.f. Cake made of besan or the meal of pulse.
more: (from بيسن) s. f A cake made of pease-meal.

def: s.m.f. (?), A small species of falcon; merlin; sparrow-hawk (syn. śikrā. Forbes, on what authority I cannot discover, states that it is the female of the species called dhotī).
more: n. f. A falcon; the hen-bird called dhotī ( Fālco nisus ). (?)
s. m. A kind of falcon. (Falco nisus).

def: s.m.f. (?), A small species of falcon; merlin; sparrow-hawk (syn. śikrā. Forbes, on what authority I cannot discover, states that it is the female of the species called dhotī).
s.f. Prostitute, harlot, courtesan; crafty or cunning woman.
more: n. f. S. वेश्या From विषि to attach. 1. Prostitute; harlot; whore; courtesan. 1. Banī phir beswā, khole phir kesvā . Prov. Your hair loose down, a woman of the town. 2. Beswā satī, na kāgā jati . Prov. Nor harlot satī pure, nor crow a jatī pure. 2. A crafty or cunning woman.

def: s.f. = beswā, q.v. s.f. Prostitute, harlot, courtesan; crafty or cunning woman.
more: s. f. A courtezan, harlot, prostitute.

def: s.f. Cane, ratan, Calamus rotang; blow or stroke with a cane
more: n. f. S. वेत्र P. bed. Reed; cane; ratan; Calamus rotang .
s. f. A cane, a ratan. (Calamus rotang).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بیت (bayt), from Arabic بَيْت (bayt).

def: s.m. Son, boy, child; a form of address used by faqīrs towards their ćelās or disciples
more: s. m. A son, a child. بيٿاکرلينا beṭā kar lenā, To adopt a son.
subs: betā kar-lenā, v.t. To adopt a son (syn. god lenā)
s.m. A father who survives his son
s.m. (f. -ī), Grandfather; a man who has children
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi بیٿا (byṭa), from Prakrit 𑀩𑀺𑀝𑁆𑀝 (biṭṭa)., further etymology unknown. Perhaps inherited from Sanskrit बटु (baṭu, “boy, lad, chap”).

def: adj. Agitated; impatient ( be-chain ).

n. f. Agitation; restlessness. Yeh be-tābī, yeh be-khuābī, yeh be-chainī dikhātā hai, Na ghar meṅ dil merā lagtā, na sahrā mujh ko bhātā hai . On separation. Nazīr. Unquiet, sleepless, restless grown to that degree, Nor home, nor lonely wood distasteful pleaseth me.
adv. A. حشی he breathed short. Recklessly; furiously; headlong. Be-tahāshā bhāgā jātā hai .
s. f. Innocence.
s.m. Sprite, goblin (especially one that is supposed to haunt cemeteries and animate corpses); a dead body occupied and animated by a goblin
more: n. m. S. वेताल A dead body occupied and animated by an evil spirit.
s. m. 1. A dead body occupied and animated by an evil spirit. 2. Name of a demon.
subs: s.f. Name of a work in Hindī (so called because it contains twenty-five tales related by a baitāl).

def: s.m. Hire, wages, pittance, reward; profession, livelihood, subsistence; silver.
s.m. = beṭnan, q.v.
adv. Badly. See be-ḍhab .
adj. Unmannerly, rude: see بيطرح be-t̤araḥ, under P. بي be.
s.f. (?), Sandy unproductive soil.
s.m. The envelope in which cloth, lace, &c. purchased is folded up, and to which the purchaser is entitled; pack-cloth, wrapper.
more: s. m. The envelope, in which cloth purchased is folded up, to which the purchaser is entitled; packcloth, wrapper.

def: n. m. S. वेष्टन from वेष्ट् to surround. A pack-cloth; the envelope in which cloth, lace, etc. purchased is folded up, and to which the purchaser is entitled.
s.f. Daughter, girl
more: s. f. A daughter. بيج bīj, s. m. 1. (for वीज ) Seed. 2. (for वीर्य्य ) Sperma genitale (viri aut mulieris).
subs: (-ko), To give one's daughter (to, in marriage)
wety: Feminine of بیٹا (beṭā).

def: n. f. A daughter. 1. Ittī sī biṭyā, savā gaz kī chuṭyā . Riddle. Ans. Sūī . Needle. The daughter but a mite, her hair a yard and one-fourth quite. beṭī behoār , n. m. Intermarriage; matrimonial alliance. beṭī denā , v. n. To give one's daughter in marriage. Jis ne beṭī dī, us ne sab kuchh dīyā . Prov. Saying. Who gives his daughter gives all. beṭī roṭī , n. f. lit . Abusing a man's daughter and calling him a beggar. Calling names; foul language; abuse. beṭī roṭī karnā , v. a. To call names; abuse; insult. beṭī-wālā , n. m. beṭī-wālī , n. f. The father or mother of the bride.
s.f. = biṭiyā; beṭī, qq.v. s.f. Little daughter; daughter.
s.m. Cane; willow
subs: s.f. Allowance for mats or wicker works for packing goods.

def: s.m. = biṭwā, q.v.
s.f. Widow ( = P. بيوه bewa, q.v.).
more: s. f. A widow. See بيوه bewa.

def: s.f. Widow ( = bewā).
more: n. f. Z. veva . A widow (بدهوا).
s. f. A widow. See بيوا bewā.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بیوَه (bēwa). Doublet of وِدَھوا (vidhavā) and بِدھوا (bidhvā).

def: adj. Immodest; shameless. Baṛā be-wahdat hai .
s.f. Widowhood ( = bewā-pā, bewā-pan)
adj. Unseasonable.
s.m. The vehicle or self-moving car of a Hindū deity; &c. ( = bimān q.v.).
more: s. m. 1. The vehicle or self-moving car of a Hindū deity: see بمان bimān. 2. A (Hindū) bier.

def: s.m. State or situation of widowhood; widowhood.
s.m. State or situation of widowhood; widowhood.
s.m. (local) = byorā, q.v. s.m. Difference, distinction; account, explanation, history, circumstantial or detailed account ( = tafṣīl); news, tidings, intelligence; day-book
subs: byorā karnā (-kā), To relate, describe; to inform, intimate
beʼore-wār, adj. & adv. Circumstantial, detailed, explicit, distinct, clear, plain; — circumstantially, in detail, explicitly, &c. (syn. tafṣīl-wār; biʼt-tafṣīl).

def: n. m. S. हृ to take. 1. Dealing; traffic; trade; transaction. 1. Ahāre, behvāre lajjā na kāre . Prov. To eat and traffic are no shame. 2. Kāyā rākhe dharam, aur pūṅjī rākhe beohār . Prov. Religion in the body dwells, as trade in capital. 2. Business; negotiation; correspondence; intercourse. 3. Calling; trade; profession; moneylending. 4. Usage; practice; custom; procedure; manner of proceeding; course. 1. Desā desā chāl, kulā kulā beohār . Prov. The customs of countries differ as the practice of families. bevhār karnā , v. n. To negotiate; to do or transact business; treat; deal with; bargain; trade; set up in business; contract. des-bevhār , n. m. The custom of the country.
adj. 1. Wonted; customary; usual. 2. Mercantile; commercial. bevhārī , n. 1. The party engaged in any affair. 2. A trader; merchant; money lender.
s.f. Widow ( = bewa)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بِیبِی (bī-bī, “lady”).

def: part. (of beḵẖtan; rt. Zend vij; S. vij or vić), Sifting; diffusing, dispersing (used in comp.).
adj. See s.v. be. s.m. The letter or sound b.
more: adj. Displeased ( nā-rāz ); offended; angry ( khafā ). Haq kahne se ahmaq be-zār . Prov. Truth angers fools.
adj. Displeased, angry, out of humour, disgusted: ( vulg. ) sick.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بیزَار (bēzār).

def: s. f. (dakh.) See بيزاري bezār-ī.
s. f. Anger, bad humour, displeasure.
n. prop. A Persian hero, son of (گيو) Gew, and of a sister of Rustam.
s.m. The letter or sound bh.
s.f. Sudden bursting forth of flame, or steam, &c.; forcible expulsion of water from a fountain or pipe; sudden emission of stench or offensive smell.
more: n. f. H. bhāp steam. 1. A sudden burst of flame. 2. A sudden emission of stench, stink, or bad smell.

def: v.n. To be heated; to simmer, boil, bubble; to give out steam; to fume; to catch fire, to burn, be burnt or scalded; to burst into flame, blaze up suddenly; to be enraged or exasperated, fly into a passion, to rush on with rage; to run with great speed (a horse); to roar (as a tiger); to be poured out, gush forth: — bhabak-jānā = bhabaknā.
more: v. n. 1. To become hot; to be heated, overboiled. 2. To simmer; boil; give out steam. 3. To burst into flame; blaze up suddenly. Bārūd bhabak uṭṭhī . 4. To be burnt, scalded. Hin. Hāth bhabhak gayā . 5. To flare up; fire up; rage; fret and fume; turn red with anger. 6. To roar; thunder. Sher bhabkā . 7. To be poured out, thrown away copiously. E.
v. n. 1. To be enraged, to rush on with rage. 2. To catch fire. 3. To run with great rapidity (a horse).

def: s.m. A grass of which rope is made; a species of nettle; — light black soil; — name of a large forest at the foot of the Sewalik hills.
more: n. m. 1. Light black soil. 2. A grass of which rope is made.

def: s.m. = bhabbhar, q.v.
more: n. m. H. bhaṛbhaṛ a confused noise. 1. Noise; tumult; uproar. 2. Panic; flight; stampede. bhabbaṛ paṛnā , v. n. 1. To be panic-struck. 2. To take flight.

def: adj. Fat, corpulent.
s.f. = bhabak, q.v. s.f. Sudden bursting forth of flame, or steam, &c.; forcible expulsion of water from a fountain or pipe; sudden emission of stench or offensive smell.
more: s. f. 1. Sudden bursting forth of flame. 2. Forcible expulsion of water from a fountain or pipe. 3. Smell arising suddenly.

def: v.n. = bhabaknā, q.v. v.n. To be heated; to simmer, boil, bubble; to give out steam; to fume; to catch fire, to burn, be burnt or scalded; to burst into flame, blaze up suddenly; to be enraged or exasperated, fly into a passion, to rush on with rage; to run with great speed (a horse); to roar (as a tiger); to be poured out, gush forth: — bhabak-jānā = bhabaknā.
more: v. n. 1. To simmer, to bubble. 2. To emit steam, to fume, to boil.

def: s.m. Disquietude, solicitude ( = khaṭkā), dread, alarm; panic, stampede; tumult, uproar
more: s. m. Solicitude, alarm. بهبهرپڙنا bhabhar-paṛnā, v. n. To be alarmed, to be frightened.
subs: (-meṅ), To be alarmed; to be panic-stricken; to take to flight.

def: v.n. To be solicitous; to be alarmed or frightened, to take fright (cf. bhabhar paṛnā).
more: v. n. To be solicitous, to be alarmed, to be frightened.

ety: S. भ्रातृ+वधू; or H. bhāʼī+bī = bī-bī
def: s.f. Brother's wife, sister-in-law (syn. bhāwaj, bhaujāʼī, bhaujī).
v.n. To swell (particularly the face = bharbharānā, q.v.); to feel doubt or solicitude, &c. ( = bhabharnā, q.v.).
more: v. n. To swell (particularly, the face). See بهربهرانا bharbharānā.

def: adj. Flaming, blazing, ablaze; red (as a live coal); white, shining, bright, glowing, refulgent, splendid, beautiful; — s.m. Blaze, flame; explosion
more: 1. s. m. Blaze, flame, explosion. 2. adj. Red (as a coal, sometimes applied to the splendour of a beloved personʼs countenance); splendid, beautiful.
subs: v.n. To turn red (or livid) with rage.

def: s.f. Ashes of cowdung, &c. which Hindū devotees rub on their bodies (in imitation of Śiva); ashes from a shrine with which Hindū worshippers smear their foreheads.
more: s. f. Ashes of cow-dung which faḳīrs rub over their bodies.

def: adj. = bhāvī, q.v. s.m. Future time, the future
subs: adj. Dependent on, or subject to, the future.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi بھابی (bhaby /⁠bhābī⁠/) (c. 1697) and Middle Hindi بھابھی (bhabhy /⁠bhābhī⁠/), from Prakrit *𑀪𑀸𑀩𑁆𑀪 (*bhābbha), from baby talk. Cognate with Punjabi بھابی (bhābī).

ety: S. भ्रातृ+वधू; or H. bhāʼī+bī = bī-bī
def: s.f. Brother's wife, sister-in-law (syn. bhāwaj, bhaujāʼī, bhaujī).
more: n. f. S. भातूबधू, H. bhā , Cont. of bhāī brother, bī , Cont. of bahū wife. Brother's wife; sister-in-law (on the brother's side).
adj. Future, about to be, predestined.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi بھابی (bhaby /⁠bhābī⁠/) (c. 1697) and Middle Hindi بھابھی (bhabhy /⁠bhābhī⁠/), from Prakrit *𑀪𑀸𑀩𑁆𑀪 (*bhābbha), from baby talk. Cognate with Punjabi بھابی (bhābī).

def: s.m. Blast from a furnace; alembic, still; — a kind of drinking vessel with a large mouth.
more: n. m. 1. A still. 2. A blast from a furnace.
s. m. 1. (from بهاپهـ Sans. वाष्प ) An alembick, a still. 2. A kind of drinking vessel with a large mouth.

def: v.t. To heat, kindle, light; to cause to boil, &c.; to cause to blaze up suddenly; to provoke, enrage, exasperate; to excite, stimulate; to spur a horse; &c. (see bhabaknā).
more: (caus. of بهبکنا) v. a. 1. To kindle, to light. 2. To provoke, to exasperate, to enrage. 3. To spur a horse.

def: s.f. Threat, menace.
more: n. f. Threat; menace; intimidation. bandar yā gīdaṛ bhabkī , n. f. The threatening look of a monkey or jackal. Ye bandar bhabkī kisī aur ko denā .

def: s.f. = bhabhūt, q.v. s.f. Ashes of cowdung, &c. which Hindū devotees rub on their bodies (in imitation of Śiva); ashes from a shrine with which Hindū worshippers smear their foreheads.
adj. = bhabhūkā, q.v. adj. Flaming, blazing, ablaze; red (as a live coal); white, shining, bright, glowing, refulgent, splendid, beautiful; — s.m. Blaze, flame; explosion
more: adj. 1. Red (as coal). 2. White; shining; bright; refulgent; most beautiful. Kuchh āṅkhoṅ meṅ narmī, woh sādā sā ḍhaṅg, Woh haṅstā sā mukhṛā, bhabūkā sā raṅg . Sohan Lāl. bhabūkā honā , v. n. To turn red with rage. Ye sun-kar woh sholā bhabūkā huī, Lagī kahne, haë haë! balā kyā huī! Mīr Hasan.
subs: v.n. To turn red (or livid) with rage.

def: s.f. = bhabhūt, q.v. s.f. Ashes of cowdung, &c. which Hindū devotees rub on their bodies (in imitation of Śiva); ashes from a shrine with which Hindū worshippers smear their foreheads.
more: n. f. S. बिभूति. Ashes from a shrine rubbed on their foreheads by Hindū worshippers; ashes from their own fires which ascetics or jogīs rub over their bodies. bhabūt ramānā, malnā, yā lagānā , v. n. To rub ashes over the body.

def: s.f. = bhabhūt, q.v. s.f. Ashes of cowdung, &c. which Hindū devotees rub on their bodies (in imitation of Śiva); ashes from a shrine with which Hindū worshippers smear their foreheads.
adj. Alarmed, startled, staggered, amazed, stunned, aghast, scared
subs: v.n. To be astonished, amazed, staggered, &c.

def: v.n. To be astonished or amazed, &c. = bhaćak rahnā, q.v.s.v. bhaćak. adj. Alarmed, startled, staggered, amazed, stunned, aghast, scared
more: v. n. To be astonished or amazed.
subs: v.n. To be astonished, amazed, staggered, &c.

def: s.m. Sphere of the stars; circle of constellations, the zodiac.
s.m. = bhakshaṇ, q.v. s.m. Eating, feeding, devouring, gormandizing; food, meal, victuals

To be clean or purified by shaving one's head and beard, etc. after the demise of a relative, or in a holy place. 2. ( Slang ). To be robbed or swept clean. Āj nīlām meṅ unkā bhadrā ho gayā .

s.m. Part, portion, member, share, lot, division; allotment, partition, distribution, apportionment; share in a partnership, or of an inheritance; share in kind; (in Arith.) division; tax, duty (as being the share of government); good portion, fortunate lot, good fortune, luck; fortune, lot, fate, destiny; — part of any whole, a fraction; a degree, or 360th part of the circumference of a circle
more: n. m. S. भाग्य from भज् to share. 1. Partition; apportionment; separate portion; a share in a partnership; a share or portion of an inheritance; a share in kind; the share of the Government. 2. ( Math .) Division (A. taqsīm ). 3. Fortune; lot; destiny; fate. Apnā apnā bhāg . 4. Good fortune; luck; prosperity. 1. Pher same ehu bane, na bane, gorī, ab-ke to khel le phāg rī; Dīn Dayāl se pritam kahāṅ mileṅ, dhan dhan apne bhāg rī ! Holī. Such luck may ne'er return, Love's feast enjoy we while we may, Like Krishn kind a Love where find, how blessed our fate to-day! 2. Rājā rūṭhegā apnā suhāg legā, kyā kisī kā bhāg legā? Prov. The offended king may put away his wife, but he cannot change her fate. 3. Nīk nīk more bhāg, ek ek machhalyā kī do do machhalyā . E. Prov. Good is my fate, for every fish doth two create. (Every rupee makes two). bhāg-bharā, bhāg-bharī , adj. 1. Fortunate; lucky; propi…
s. m. 1. Share, part, portion. 2. (or भाग्य ) Destiny, fate, fortune, lot. بهاگ بهروسا bhāg-bharosā, Consolation.
